premises: For any statements A and B, if it is possible that (A and B) is true, then it is possible that A is true, and it is possible that B is true. It is possible that (Snowing and Sunny) is true.
conclusion: It is possible that Snowing is true.
label: True

premises: For any number N, if N is necessarily prime, then N cannot be factored into two smaller integers greater than 1. Number 7 is necessarily prime.
conclusion: Number 7 can be factored into two smaller integers greater than 1.
label: False

premises: For any entity E, if E is necessarily a mammal, then E is necessarily warm-blooded. Entity Whiskers is necessarily a mammal.
conclusion: Entity Whiskers is necessarily warm-blooded.
label: True

premises: For any rule R, if R is necessarily true, then R is true in every accessible possible world. Rule 'No_Two_Solid_Objects_Occupy_Same_Space' is necessarily true. World Gamma is an accessible possible world.
conclusion: Rule 'No_Two_Solid_Objects_Occupy_Same_Space' is true in World Gamma.
label: True

premises: For any property P, if an object necessarily has P, and the object loses P, the object ceases to exist. Object 'Triangle' necessarily has the property 'Three_Sided'. Object 'Triangle' loses the property 'Three_Sided'.
conclusion: Object 'Triangle' ceases to exist.
label: True

premises: For any action A, if it is necessarily true that A causes B, and A physically occurs, then B necessarily occurs. It is necessarily true that (Dropping_Glass causes Glass_Breaking). Dropping_Glass physically occurs.
conclusion: Glass_Breaking necessarily occurs.
label: True

premises: For any statement S, if S is possible, the negation of S is not necessarily true. Statement 'Economy_Recovers' is possible.
conclusion: The negation of Statement 'Economy_Recovers' is necessarily true.
label: False

premises: For any path P, if P is necessarily the shortest path, no other path P2 can possibly be shorter than P. Path Route_66 is necessarily the shortest path. Path Route_77 is another path.
conclusion: Path Route_77 can possibly be shorter than Path Route_66.
label: False

premises: For any entities X and Y, X overlaps Y if and only if there exists some entity Z such that Z is a part of X and Z is a part of Y. Entity Alpha and Entity Beta share no common parts.
conclusion: Entity Alpha overlaps Entity Beta.
label: False

premises: For any abstract concepts X and Y, if X is a sub-concept of Y, X is considered a mereological part of Y. Sub-concepts do not necessarily have less mass than their parent concepts. Concept 'Poodle' is a sub-concept of Concept 'Dog'.
conclusion: The physical mass of Concept 'Poodle' is strictly less than the physical mass of Concept 'Dog'.
label: Uncertain

premises: For any entities X and Y, if X is a mereological sum of a set of parts, and Y is formed by the exact same set of parts arranged differently, X is identical to Y. Statue_A is a mereological sum of a set of clay pieces. Lump_B is formed by the exact same set of clay pieces arranged differently.
conclusion: Statue_A is identical to Lump_B.
label: True

premises: For any substance S, if S is a homogeneous liquid, any physically extracted portion of S is a proper part of S. Portion P1 is physically extracted from homogeneous liquid Water_Tank.
conclusion: Portion P1 is a proper part of Water_Tank.
label: True

premises: For any regions R1 and R2, if R1 is a non-tangential proper part of R2, R1 is a part of R2 and R1 does not share any boundary with the exterior of R2. The Core is a non-tangential proper part of the Reactor.
conclusion: The Core shares a boundary with the exterior of the Reactor.
label: False

premises: For any object O, if O is fundamentally emergent, O possesses properties that none of its proper parts possess. Object Brain is fundamentally emergent. Neuron N is a proper part of Object Brain.
conclusion: Neuron N possesses all the properties of Object Brain.
label: False

premises: For any legal document D, if clause C is a severed part of D, C is no longer legally binding, but D remains binding. Clause 4 is a severed part of Document Contract_V.
conclusion: Clause 4 is legally binding.
label: False

premises: For any text T, if paragraph P is a semantic part of T, the meaning of T depends on P. Paragraph Intro is a semantic part of Text Essay_Final.
conclusion: The meaning of Text Essay_Final depends on Paragraph Intro.
label: True

premises: For any planetary system S, if moon M is a part of S, M orbits a planet that is also a part of S. Moon Europa is a part of Planetary system Solar. Planet Jupiter is a part of Planetary system Solar.
conclusion: Moon Europa orbits Planet Jupiter.
label: Uncertain

premises: For any symphony S, if movement M is a part of S, M is meant to be played in a specific sequence. Movement Allegro is a part of Symphony No_9.
conclusion: Movement Allegro is meant to be played in a specific sequence.
label: True
